title: Implement getDirectAssignment() and field whitelist in CategoryMappingService
description: Add new `getDirectAssignment(transaction)` method returning `{assigned: true, category, mappingName, matchedKeyword, reason}` when an enabled direct-assign mapping matches, or `{assigned: false}` on no-match. Add `#MAPPING_FIELDS` whitelist (`['name','targetCategory','keywords','enabled','directAssign']`) and apply in both `addMapping()` and `updateMapping()` to prevent arbitrary field injection via spread-merge. Store `directAssign: Boolean(value ?? false)` when present in input.
notes: Reference implementation in docs/engineering/architecture-us0007.md §3.1. Method must skip mappings where `directAssign` is falsy. Reuses existing `#getTransactionSearchText()` and `#looselyMatchesKeyword()` helpers. The whitelist is a behavior change (arbitrary extra keys from req.body previously persisted) but only the 5 whitelisted fields are ever sent by the frontend.
verification_steps: Add `getDirectAssignment()` method at end of service, immediately after `getAiHint()`.
Update `updateMapping()`: use `this.#stripFields(updates)` before spread-merge. When `'directAssign' in clean`, coerce to Boolean.
Existing 18/18 tests still green after the change (no functional change to AI-hint path).
Manual smoke: `addMapping({directAssign: true})` persists field; `updateMapping(id, {malicious: true})` does not persist `malicious`.
